summary: Russian forces reported advancing toward Kramatorsk while Ukrainian defenses destroyed a strategic rail bridge over the North Crimean Canal. Russian ballistic missile threats were noted from the northeast and Iskander-M systems. Ukraine reported power outages in Sevastopol and Simferopol after attacks on electricity infrastructure.
key_points: Russian troops are advancing toward Kramatorsk from multiple directions, according to a TASS report (Source: @tass_agency/382126).
A rail bridge over the North Crimean Canal was destroyed, cutting a key logistical route for Russian forces (Source: @UkraineNow/82133).
Sevastopol and Simferopol suffered electricity outages after attacks on power substations (Source: @eRadarrua/97954).
Ukrainian air defenses intercepted two UAVs over Sevastopol (Source: @tass_agency/382121).
A missile strike on Kryvyi Rih killed three people and injured 23, damaging industrial infrastructure (Source: @UkraineNow/82131).
Russian ballistic missile threats were reported from the northeast, including potential Iskander-M launches (Source: @eRadarrua/97941; @mon1tor_ua/68022).
Ukrainian forces reported repeated guided aerial bomb attacks on Kharkiv and Donetsk regions (Source: @kpszsu/66100; @kpszsu/66125).

sections.executive_summary: Russian forces are pushing toward Kramatorsk from several axes, indicating a renewed offensive in the east. Ukraine has successfully destroyed a strategic rail bridge over the North Crimean Canal, disrupting Russian logistics. Simultaneously, power infrastructure in Sevastopol and Simferopol was knocked out, and missile strikes in Kryvyi Rih caused civilian casualties.

points: Ukrainian forces destroyed the rail bridge over the North Crimean Canal, a vital logistical artery for Russian supply movements across the peninsula (Source: @UkraineNow/82133).
Power substations in Sevastopol and Simferopol were hit, leaving both cities without electricity and creating civilian hardship (Source: @eRadarrua/97954).
A missile attack on Kryvyi Rih resulted in three deaths and 23 injuries, with damage to industrial facilities (Source: @UkraineNow/82131).
Ukrainian air defenses shot down two UAVs attempting to approach Sevastopol, preventing further aerial threats (Source: @tass_agency/382121).
Repeated guided aerial bomb strikes were reported on Kharkiv and Donetsk regions, indicating sustained Ukrainian counter‑air activity (Source: @kpszsu/66100; @kpszsu/66125).
Russian troops claim confident progress toward Kramatorsk, attacking from multiple directions (Source: @tass_agency/382126).
A threat of ballistic missile launches from the northeast was announced, including possible Iskander‑M systems (Source: @eRadarrua/97941; @mon1tor_ua/68022).
Russian forces reported a reactive Shahed drone approaching Kremenchuk from the east (Source: @mon1tor_ua/68030).
Russian tactical aviation conducted guided aerial bomb attacks on Kharkiv and Donetsk, targeting urban and industrial sites (Source: @kpszsu/66100; @kpszsu/66125).
A Russian fuel truck evaded a Ukrainian drone, showing continued logistical movements under threat (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31094).
Ukrainian reports detail UAV activity over northern Dnipropetrovsk near Yuriyivka and Zarichne, heading westward (Source: @kpszsu/66134).
Multiple UAVs were observed over Kharkiv region, including attacks on industrial sites and city areas (Source: @kpszsu/66100; @kpszsu/66125).
Russian forces claim increased activity on the Lyman, Kupiansk, South and North Slobozhansky directions, suggesting broader eastern pressure (Source: @UkraineNow/82138).
Guided aerial bomb strikes were recorded over Donetsk, indicating continued Russian air operations in the eastern sector (Source: @kpszsu/66125).
Ukrainian intelligence warned of a potential new offensive from Belarus if Russian troop numbers rise, highlighting strategic concerns on the eastern flank (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31096).
UAV activity was reported in northern Dnipropetrovsk, including three UAVs near Samara and Podhorodne (Source: @eRadarrua/97940).
A UAV was sighted over northern Cherkasy heading toward Korsun‑Shevchenkovsky (Source: @eRadarrua/97940).
Ukrainian radar noted a MiG‑31K launch from Savaslyka, capable of carrying the X‑47M2 Kinzhal hypersonic missile (Source: @mon1tor_ua/68026).
A missile was reported in Vinnytsia heading toward Voronovytsia (Source: @kpszsu/66107).
UAVs were observed over northern Sumy moving toward Chernihiv, indicating cross‑border aerial activity (Source: @kpszsu/66105).

Power outages spread across multiple districts of Crimea, including Yevpatoria, Krasnoperekopsk, Sakovsky, Dzhankoy, and Krasnogvardeysky, after attacks on the regional power grid (Source: @UkraineNow/82129).
Russian forces are allegedly transporting infected livestock to burial sites near Aksanija‑Nova, Skadovsk, and Zalizny Port, raising concerns of biological contamination (Source: @UkraineNow/82135).
A Russian missile strike on Kryvyi Rih caused civilian casualties and damaged industrial infrastructure (Source: @UkraineNow/82131).
Ukrainian forces reported a guided aerial bomb attack on the temporary occupied Kerch, targeting a thermal power plant (Source: @UkraineNow/82129).
Russian ballistic missile threats from the northeast could affect southern Ukrainian territories (Source: @eRadarrua/97941).
Ukrainian air defenses shot down two UAVs over Sevastopol, preventing further aerial incursions (Source: @tass_agency/382121).
Russian tactical aviation launched guided aerial bombs on Kharkiv and Donetsk, causing damage to urban areas (Source: @kpszsu/66100; @kpszsu/66125).
A MiG‑31K fighter took off from Savaslyka, capable of carrying the X‑47M2 Kinzhal hypersonic missile (Source: @mon1tor_ua/68026).
Ukrainian radar reported a reactive Shahed drone approaching Kremenchuk from the east (Source: @mon1tor_ua/68030).
Multiple UAVs were intercepted or reported over various regions, including northern Dnipropetrovsk, Cherkasy, and Sumy (Sources: @eRadarrua/97940; @kpszsu/66134; @kpszsu/66105).

key_developments: Russian forces claim multi‑directional advance toward Kramatorsk, suggesting a coordinated eastern offensive (Source: @tass_agency/382126).
Ukrainian sabotage destroyed the rail bridge over the North Crimean Canal, disrupting Russian logistical flows (Source: @UkraineNow/82133).
Power substations in Sevastopol and Simferopol were hit, causing widespread electricity outages in Crimea (Source: @eRadarrua/97954).
A missile strike on Kryvyi Rih resulted in three civilian deaths and 23 injuries, highlighting ongoing strike risk to civilian centers (Source: @UkraineNow/82131).
Russian ballistic missile threats, including potential Iskander‑M launches, were announced from the northeast (Source: @eRadarrua/97941; @mon1tor_ua/68022).
Ukrainian air defenses intercepted two UAVs over Sevastopol, demonstrating effective anti‑air capabilities (Source: @tass_agency/382121).
Russian tactical aviation conducted guided aerial bomb attacks on Kharkiv and Donetsk, indicating sustained air pressure on eastern fronts (Source: @kpszsu/66100; @kpszsu/66125).
sections.threat_assessment: Russian forces are intensifying pressure on the eastern front, with reported advances toward Kramatorsk and increased use of guided aerial bombs in Kharkiv and Donetsk. Simultaneously, ballistic missile threats from the northeast, including potential Iskander‑M launches, raise the risk of high‑impact strikes across multiple fronts. Ukrainian sabotage of the North Crimean Canal rail bridge and successful air defenses over Crimea demonstrate Kyiv's capability to disrupt Russian logistics and mitigate aerial threats, but civilian infrastructure remains vulnerable, as shown by power outages in Crimea and missile damage in Kryvyi Rih.
sections.regional_response: Ukrainian authorities have mobilized air defense assets to intercept UAVs and reported successful engagements over Sevastopol and other regions. Civilian emergency services are responding to casualties in Kryvyi Rih, while local administrations in Crimea are managing widespread power outages. International observers note the strategic significance of the rail bridge destruction, potentially prompting increased support for Ukrainian logistical interdiction efforts.
sections.intelligence_notes: Monitoring should focus on the reported northeast ballistic missile launch sites and any movement of Iskander‑M units, as well as the status of the destroyed rail bridge to assess Russian logistical adaptation. UAV activity remains high across northern and eastern oblasts, requiring continued radar and interception resources. Biological threat claims in Crimea warrant verification and possible bio‑hazard containment measures.
